commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

commit.message:
feat(hub): add HTTP bridge for cross-machine war room coordination

Enable Mac ↔ WSL war room connectivity via HTTP bridge server.
SQLite can't be shared across filesystems, so the bridge exposes
all war room operations over REST. Remote terminals set PROWLR_HUB_URL
and the MCP server automatically routes through the bridge client.

- bridge.py: FastAPI server exposing all 13 war room operations
- remote_client.py: stdlib HTTP client (no external deps)
- mcp_server.py: auto-detects remote mode via PROWLR_HUB_URL env var
- db.py: fix check_same_thread for FastAPI thread pool compatibility
- 12 new E2E tests verifying full bridge → engine chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add vanilla-service subproject for per-investor DB ledger (#128)

* Add optional omnibus DB ledger service implementation

Port the Go vanilla adapter's per-investor account/transaction ledger
to the Node.js skeleton as an opt-in service layer. Adapters that use
a single omnibus account (e.g., Ethereum) can enable this to maintain
per-investor balance segregation in PostgreSQL while delegating
external operations to an adapter-provided OmnibusDelegate.

Key components:
- OmnibusDelegate interface in adapter-models (contract for external ops)
- OmnibusStorage: atomic CTE-based PostgreSQL ledger (credit, debit,
  lock, unlock, move, unlockAndMove, unlockAndDebit) with idempotency
- OmnibusTokenService, OmnibusEscrowService, OmnibusCommonService
- createOmnibusServices() factory for opt-in usage
- Migration for omnibus_accounts and omnibus_transactions tables
- 33 tests covering all operations, constraints, edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump skeleton version to 0.27.3-rc.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump adapter-models to 0.27.2-rc.1 for OmnibusDelegate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update skeleton dep on adapter-models to ^0.27.2-rc.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update skeleton package-lock.json for adapter-models 0.27.2-rc.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove omnibus_ prefix from DB table names

Rename omnibus_accounts → accounts and omnibus_transactions → transactions
within the ledger_adapter schema. The schema already provides namespacing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Change createOmnibusServices to accept StorageConfig instead of Pool

Use the same connection string pattern as the workflow layer so adapters
don't need to create a separate Pool for omnibus storage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump skeleton to 0.27.3-rc.2

Includes table rename (remove omnibus_ prefix) and StorageConfig refactor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove RC suffixes: adapter-models 0.27.2, skeleton 0.27.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update skeleton package-lock.json for adapter-models 0.27.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix omnibus receipt/storage correctness issues

- Receipt ID uses persisted omnibus tx ID; external tx ID goes in transactionDetails
- Forward externalTransactionId to delegate.generateReceipt
- Store operation_type in transaction details for valid API receipts
- Use tx returned by debit/unlockAndDebit directly in redeem (no re-query)
- Include asset_type in accounts PK and all WHERE clauses

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add vanilla-service subproject for per-investor DB ledger

Extract omnibus ledger code into a standalone `vanilla-service` package
(`@owneraio/finp2p-vanilla-service`) with its own migrations, tests, and
publish workflow. Revert skeleton and adapter-models to master state —
all ledger bookkeeping now lives in vanilla-service with no coupling to
the skeleton framework.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove onAssetCreated and generateReceipt from ExternalTransferDelegate

These optional hooks added unnecessary complexity. Asset creation now
always generates a local tokenId. Receipts are always built from the
DB transaction record.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Consolidate services into single VanillaServiceImpl

Combine TokenServiceImpl, EscrowServiceImpl, and CommonServiceImpl into
a unified VanillaServiceImpl class. Rework delegate interfaces: rename
ExternalTransferDelegate to PayoutDelegate with lock-transfer-debit
pattern, add optional EscrowDelegate for external escrow operations,
and introduce DelegateResult discriminated union for explicit
success/failure handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add MappingService interface, delegates, and refine vanilla service

- Add OwnerMapping type and MappingService interface to adapter-models
- Add optional AssetDelegate for custom asset creation
- Make PayoutDelegate optional (fail on external transfer if missing)
- Add BIGSERIAL id to accounts table, account_mappings table to migration
- Move buildReceipt to utils, remove Pool dependency from VanillaServiceImpl
- Implement MappingService on VanillaServiceImpl (get/list/save/delete)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add InboundTransferHook for inbound transfer detection during plan approval and instruction proposals

Introduces InboundTransferHook plugin interface with two notification hooks:
- onPlannedInboundTransfer: called during plan approval when an inbound transfer is detected
- onInboundTransfer: called during instruction proposal with the execution result

The skeleton detects inbound transfers (any destination type) and calls the hook.
VanillaServiceImpl implements the hook to ensureAccount on planned transfers and
credit the destination on successful inbound transfers (finId destinations only).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump versions: adapter-models 0.27.3, skeleton 0.27.4, vanilla-service 0.27.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix instruction proposal timing and test teardown robustness

- Don't reject instruction proposals when completion events aren't yet
  visible; log a warning and skip the hook instead of failing
- Remove unused RandomPortGenerator from vanilla-service test environment
- Guard afterEach teardown against undefined pool/container

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use file: protocol for adapter-models dependency in CI

CI builds adapter-models locally before skeleton/vanilla-service, so
version ranges can't resolve from the registry for unpublished versions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: deduplicate C-style and C++-style equivalent headers in auto-include

Add header-aliases module mapping 19 C↔C++ header pairs (stdio.h↔cstdio,
stdlib.h↔cstdlib, string.h↔cstring, math.h↔cmath, etc.). Auto-include,
scaffold, and code patcher now recognize equivalent headers to prevent
duplicates like both #include <stdio.h> and #include <cstdio>.

feat(hub): add ProwlrHub War Room — multi-agent coordination via MCP

SQLite-backed coordination engine that connects multiple Claude Code
terminals into a shared war room. Features atomic task claiming with
file locking, shared context for cross-agent discovery, event audit
logging, dead agent sweep, and capability-based task routing.

Components:
- engine.py: WarRoomEngine with rooms, agents, tasks, locks, events
- mcp_server.py: MCP stdio server with 13 coordination tools
- db.py: SQLite schema (7 tables, WAL mode, 5 indexes)
- SKILL.md: War Room Protocol for Claude Code behavior
- README.md: Comprehensive documentation for all skill levels
- 32 tests covering all coordination primitives

feat: memory v2 — health checks, editing hints, reindex API, and AI Memory Advisor

Add a complete memory health system with 9 health checks (MEMORY.md line count,
file sizes, stale daily logs, total size, unindexed vector search, stale index,
stale evergreen files, missing config, temporal decay disabled), editing hints,
server-side file writing, and a reindex API endpoint.

Add AI Memory Advisor panel (v2.1) to the Overview tab — streams actionable
guidance for each health check via the same Agent Optimizer pattern used on the
Costs page. Health check rows now have "How to fix", "View", and "Reindex now"
action buttons. Inline chat supports follow-up questions with full context.

Add custom roles & permissions system with DB-backed RBAC

Replaces hardcoded role-permission mapping with per-tenant custom roles.
Each role has granular Read/Write/Execute permissions across 11 resource
areas (punch, timesheet, leave, payroll, employee, rules, site, document,
report, audit, role) with own/team/all scope levels.

- New CustomRole and RolePermission tables (prisma/schema/rbac.prisma)
- Permission resolver with 60s LRU cache (permission-resolver.ts)
- Legacy map for backward compat with existing withRBAC guards
- Role management UI at /admin/roles with permission matrix editor
- Employee forms use dynamic role dropdown from DB
- Sidebar uses permission-based filtering instead of hardcoded role arrays
- Seed script creates 5 system roles and links existing employees
- Auth layer propagates customRoleId through JWT/session

commit.message:
refactor: eliminate P3 hardcoded patterns with registry-based architecture (TDD)

Replace 6 hardcoded patterns with registry/metadata-driven alternatives:
- Phase A: Remove 115-line BLOCK_LEVELS constant (use BlockSpecRegistry.getLevel);
  extract meta-concept generators from if-else chain in code-generator
- Phase B: Remove STATEMENT_TO_EXPRESSION map (use expressionCounterpart in
  RenderMapping); move stripScaffoldNodes to cpp-scaffold-filter.ts
- Phase C: Remove CATEGORY_DEFS constant; externalize to cpp/toolbox-categories.ts
- Phase D: Replace interpreter 47-case switch with ConceptExecutorRegistry;
  split into 9 executor modules (literals, variables, operators, control-flow,
  functions, io, arrays, pointers, mutations)

All 1659 tests pass across 95 test files.

commit.message:
fix(matlot): skip MOSYS LOTTO_VERIFICATO writes for non-tracked materials

Add _is_mosys_writable() guard so update_matlot_lotto_status() silently
skips all 6 write paths (release, withdraw, edit, bulk-release, bulk-status,
bulk-uwagi) for materials whose CODICE_MATERIALE does not start with a
tracked prefix (t/T, I, HPR).  External material types are managed outside
LINEA and must not have their LOTTO_VERIFICATO overwritten.

Also add update_matlot_pwz.py one-off script to mark PWZ batches as
verified (LOTTO_VERIFICATO='S') with dry-run and confirmation prompt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add full training pipeline: data prep, frequent checkpoints, SFT chat

- prepare_data.py: one-command data collection (SlimPajama, OpenWebText,
  StarCoder, Wikipedia for pre-training; OASST, Dolly, Alpaca, OpenOrca
  for chat SFT). Tokenizes into binary shards for fast I/O.

- train.py: rolling checkpoints every 250 steps (keep last 3),
  permanent saves every 2000 steps, sharded data loading,
  SFT mode with loss masking (only train on assistant responses).

- resonance/data.py: ShardedPretrainDataset (memory-mapped binary shards,
  distributed-aware), SFTDataset (chat data with ignore_index=-100).

- configs/titan_1t_sft.yaml: SFT config (lower LR, 50k steps).

- scripts/train_1t_chatbot.sh: full pipeline (pretrain -> SFT) on B200 cluster.
- scripts/prepare_data.sh: data prep on network volume.
